decision_target: Establish the canonical Node/V8 sampled-allocation byte aggregate and a warmup-separated stationarity calibration before measuring product lifecycle allocation.

frozen_protocol.candidate_aggregate: sum HeapProfiler.SamplingHeapProfile.samples[].size across the complete sampling window
frozen_protocol.rationale: The V8 inspector protocol defines each sample.size as allocation bytes attributed to that sample. Node exposes the protocol through node:inspector. node selfSize/sample equality is not a protocol invariant and must not be used as an acceptance gate.
frozen_protocol.warmup: Complete identical unmeasured warmup before HeapProfiler.startSampling.
frozen_protocol.process_isolation: Each control runs in a fresh process; keep allocated objects live until stopSampling returns.
frozen_protocol.zero_subtraction: max(0, sampled bytes for N batches minus sampled bytes for zero batches)
frozen_protocol.chronology: Write protocol and verifier before the first raw observation. Preserve every raw profile and hash. No favorable rerun.

acceptance: The verifier replays every raw profile hash and derived value.
All corrected one-batch signals exceed 1 MiB.
Every two-to-one corrected sampled-byte ratio is within 1.75 to 2.25.
Three independent one-batch observations are pairwise within the 0.80 to 1.25 stationarity band.
The method decision uses samples[].size only; node selfSize is informational.
No product, publishing clone source, or .okra state is edited by the worker.
stop_rule: Accept or reject this aggregate and stationarity protocol once. Do not measure product rows, optimize code, or claim CKR performance.
